assumptions: Traffic ~<50k/mo (not stated); file ingestion is moderate volume — sized accordingly
Files are batch uploads (CSV/JSON/Parquet), not streaming byte streams
Transformation is CPU/memory-bounded but fits within Lambda 15-min / 10 GB limits per chunk; heavy jobs noted as option
Target datastore is DynamoDB (schemaless, serverless, scales with pipeline) — relational variant called out in keyDecisions
No compliance regime stated; standard security floor applied
Idempotency key = S3 object ETag + chunk offset stored in DynamoDB conditional write
Downtime tolerance: async pipeline; individual job failures are retried via DLQ — no strict SLA stated
Cost estimates are on-demand list prices for us-east-1, presented as ranges over assumed monthly volumes. They exclude the AWS Free Tier, Savings Plans, Reserved Instances, and negotiated discounts.
Assumed traffic ~100k–1M requests/month, applied equally to all three tiers (tiers differ by robustness, not traffic). Set "expected monthly visitors" in intake to change it.

tiers.name: budget
tiers.summary: Serverless-no-VPC ETL: S3 ingest → SQS-buffered chunking → parallel Lambda transform → DynamoDB load. Zero idle floor; scales to file burst via queue buffering and Lambda concurrency.

security: alarms: DLQ depth, error rate, p99 duration, throttles
S3 data events on ingest bucket

delta: Baseline: single-region, serverless-no-VPC — $0 idle floor
SQS raw-event buffer absorbs file-upload bursts without dropping events
Splitter Lambda chunks large files so each unit is independently retried
DLQs on both queues with CW alarm on depth > 0
Lambda reserved concurrency (splitter=20, transform=50) controls drain rate
DynamoDB on-demand absorbs write spikes; conditional writes enforce idempotency
Quarantine bucket captures unparseable records without failing the job
Observability: CW Logs + golden-signal alarms → SNS → SES email on-call
tradeoffs: Single-region: a regional outage stalls the pipeline until recovery
Lambda 15-min / 10 GB per chunk — very large files require fine-grained chunking or migration to Fargate/Glue (balanced option)
DynamoDB: no ad-hoc SQL; swap to Aurora Serverless v2 if relational schema needed
No dedicated monitoring dashboard — balanced tier adds CloudWatch dashboards

costDrivers.note: S3 Standard storage, first 50TB/month tier.
PUT/COPY/POST/LIST requests per 1,000. GET/SELECT requests are ~$0.0004 per 1,000.
Data transfer out to internet, first 10TB/month tier (North America). First 1TB/month is free.
HTTPS requests ($0.0075 per 10,000). North America/Europe.
REST API on-demand ($3.50 per 1M, first 333M tier). HTTP API is cheaper (~$0.001 per 1k / $1.00 per 1M).
Compute duration: $0.0000166667 per GB-second (x86). Multiply by memoryGB x durationSeconds per invocation.
On-demand list price ($0.20 per 1M requests). Excludes Free Tier.
Standard queue requests ($0.40 per 1M). First 1M/month free. FIFO is $0.50 per 1M.
On-demand read request units ($0.125 per 1M RRU). 1 RRU = 1 strongly-consistent read up to 4KB.
On-demand write request units ($0.625 per 1M WRU). 1 WRU = 1 write up to 1KB.
CloudWatch Logs ingestion $0.50/GB (first 10GB/mo free in some accounts). Archival storage is cheaper; Insights queries extra.
Standard topic API requests ($0.50 per 1M). Delivery (HTTP/SMS/email) priced separately.
Email send: $0.10 per 1,000 emails (content). Attachment/data transfer billed separately.

tiers.name: balanced
tiers.summary: Multi-AZ, VPC-isolated target datastore, Glue option for heavy transforms, Slack alerting, and CloudWatch dashboard for operational visibility.

security: alarms: DLQ depth, error rate, p99 duration, throttles
S3 data events on ingest bucket

edges.payload: file descriptor for Glue ETL run
metrics: throughput, error rate, DLQ depth

delta: Glue ETL job added for files exceeding Lambda chunk limits (size threshold in splitter)
Dedicated SQS queue + DLQ for large-file Glue triggers
Lambda transform reserved concurrency raised to 100 for higher sustained throughput
SNS→Lambda→Slack bridge for critical-path alarm routing
CloudWatch dashboard: pipeline throughput, error rate, DLQ depth, p99 latency
VPC endpoint from Lambda to DynamoDB (no public egress for data path)
tradeoffs: Glue adds cold-start latency (~1–2 min) per large-file job — acceptable for batch, not streaming
Still single-region: a regional event stalls in-flight jobs; resilient tier adds cross-region DR
NAT gateway cost introduced if Glue job needs public internet egress (avoid by using S3/DynamoDB VPC endpoints)
No cross-region replication of DynamoDB target table — resilient tier adds Global Tables

PUT/COPY/POST/LIST requests per 1,000. GET/SELECT requests are ~$0.0004 per 1,000.

HTTPS requests ($0.0075 per 10,000). North America/Europe.
REST API on-demand ($3.50 per 1M, first 333M tier). HTTP API is cheaper (~$0.001 per 1k / $1.00 per 1M).

On-demand write request units ($0.625 per 1M WRU). 1 WRU = 1 write up to 1KB.
CloudWatch Logs ingestion $0.50/GB (first 10GB/mo free in some accounts). Archival storage is cheaper; Insights queries extra.
Standard topic API requests ($0.50 per 1M). Delivery (HTTP/SMS/email) priced separately.

tiers.name: resilient
tiers.summary: Multi-region active-passive DR: DynamoDB Global Tables, S3 cross-region replication, EventBridge cross-region routing, Route 53 health-checked failover, PagerDuty escalation, and SLO alarms.

security: alarms: DLQ depth, error rate, p99 duration, throttles
S3 data events on ingest bucket
ML anomaly band on throughput + error rate

edges.payload: file descriptor for Glue ETL run
metrics: throughput, error rate, DLQ depth

delta: DynamoDB Global Tables: active-active replication across 2 regions for target + state tables
S3 Cross-Region Replication on ingest bucket → DR region replica
EventBridge cross-region bus replaces direct S3→SQS in primary; DR region can re-replay from replica
Route 53 health-checked failover on ingest API endpoint
PagerDuty SNS topic for P1 escalation (SLO burn-rate breach)
CloudWatch anomaly detection + SLO burn-rate alarms on pipeline throughput and error rate
tradeoffs: Global Tables replication lag ~1–2 s; conditional writes still prevent double-processing but cross-region ordering is eventual
CRR adds S3 egress cost proportional to file volume — worthwhile only if RPO < 1 hr is required
EventBridge adds ~1 ms latency per event; schema registry governance overhead
Significantly higher cost floor vs balanced — justified only for pipelines with SLA-backed RPO/RTO requirements

costDrivers.note: S3 Standard storage, first 50TB/month tier.
PUT/COPY/POST/LIST requests per 1,000. GET/SELECT requests are ~$0.0004 per 1,000.
Data transfer out to internet, first 10TB/month tier (North America). First 1TB/month is free.
HTTPS requests ($0.0075 per 10,000). North America/Europe.
REST API on-demand ($3.50 per 1M, first 333M tier). HTTP API is cheaper (~$0.001 per 1k / $1.00 per 1M).
Compute duration: $0.0000166667 per GB-second (x86). Multiply by memoryGB x durationSeconds per invocation.
On-demand list price ($0.20 per 1M requests). Excludes Free Tier.
Standard queue requests ($0.40 per 1M). First 1M/month free. FIFO is $0.50 per 1M.
On-demand read request units ($0.125 per 1M RRU). 1 RRU = 1 strongly-consistent read up to 4KB.

CloudWatch Logs ingestion $0.50/GB (first 10GB/mo free in some accounts). Archival storage is cheaper; Insights queries extra.
Standard topic API requests ($0.50 per 1M). Delivery (HTTP/SMS/email) priced separately.
Email send: $0.10 per 1,000 emails (content). Attachment/data transfer billed separately.
Custom event-bus events: $1.00 per 1M events published. Pipes/schema registry priced separately.

keyDecisions.rationale: Cost Optimization + Performance Efficiency: Lambda gives $0 idle floor and scales per-chunk concurrently with no cluster management; Glue/Fargate belong in balanced/resilient where startup latency and sustained throughput justify the always-on cost.

Reliability: SQS decouples burst uploads from worker drain rate, gives at-least-once with DLQ, and prevents Lambda concurrency spikes from dropping events — a direct S3→Lambda trigger has no buffer and throttled invocations are lost.

Cost Optimization + Reliability: DynamoDB on-demand absorbs write spikes from workers without provisioned capacity and has no always-on floor; Aurora Serverless v2 is the right swap if a relational schema or SQL analytics is required — call out in balanced delta.

keyDecisions.chosen: Splitter Lambda reads S3 object, emits per-chunk SQS messages
alternativesConsidered: Single Lambda per file (hits 15-min/10 GB limit)
S3 Select for filtering before transform
keyDecisions.rationale: Reliability + Performance Efficiency: chunking prevents timeouts on large files, allows parallel workers per file, and makes each unit of work independently retryable via DLQ.

keyDecisions.chosen: Conditional write on composite key (file ETag + chunk index)
keyDecisions.rationale: Reliability: a conditional PutItem is atomic and cheap — it turns at-least-once SQS delivery into exactly-once record processing without a separate dedup store.

securityFloor: Encrypt all data at rest (KMS / service-native SSE), preferring customer-managed keys.
Enforce TLS in transit everywhere; terminate HTTPS at the edge and reject plaintext.
Least-privilege IAM: scoped per-service roles, no wildcards, no long-lived access keys.
Enable S3 Block Public Access account-wide so no bucket or object can be made public.
Keep the data tier private: VPC-bound stores in private subnets with no public route; managed/serverless stores (DynamoDB/S3) are already private via IAM and need no VPC or NAT.
Store secrets in AWS Secrets Manager / SSM Parameter Store — never in code or images.
Front public endpoints with CloudFront + AWS WAF (managed + rate-based rules); Shield Standard for L3/L4.
Enable CloudTrail plus S3/CloudFront/ALB access logs and VPC Flow Logs for audit.
